agent.system_template:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"{{ task }}"

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of {{ step_budget }} movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
agent.instance_template: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tools.name: observe
tools.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tools.name: look_around
tools.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tools.name: step
tools.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

messages.content:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Turn right to walk through the right doorway. Walk through the left doorway. Stop at the entrance to the dining room. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my current surroundings.

message.content: I'll start by observing my current surroundings.

messages.content: I see a wooden door ahead and an archway to the right. Let me look around to get a full picture before deciding.

message.content: I see a wooden door ahead and an archway to the right. Let me look around to get a full picture before deciding.

messages.content: I need to turn right to find the right doorway mentioned in the instructions. Let me turn right and observe.

message.content: I need to turn right to find the right doorway mentioned in the instructions. Let me turn right and observe.

messages.content: I can see multiple archways. Let me move forward a bit to get closer and identify the right doorway more clearly.

message.content: I can see multiple archways. Let me move forward a bit to get closer and identify the right doorway more clearly.

messages.content: I'll turn right 90° to align with that hallway view showing multiple archways.

message.content: I'll turn right 90° to align with that hallway view showing multiple archways.

messages.content: The alcove with the mirror/bench isn't a through-doorway - it's a decorative niche. Let me go back to the main junction and take the center-right archway that leads to a hallway, which seems to be the actual "right doorway".

message.content: The alcove with the mirror/bench isn't a through-doorway - it's a decorative niche. Let me go back to the main junction and take the center-right archway that leads to a hallway, which seems to be the actual "right doorway".

messages.content: This looks promising - I can see what appears to be a dining table through the archway. Let me check surroundings first.

message.content: This looks promising - I can see what appears to be a dining table through the archway. Let me check surroundings first.

messages.content: I'm very close to the wall/doorframe. Let me turn right slightly to align with the doorway opening.

message.content: I'm very close to the wall/doorframe. Let me turn right slightly to align with the doorway opening.

messages.content: I can see a room to the left with a large table and chairs - this looks like the dining room! Let me turn left to face it directly.

message.content: I can see a room to the left with a large table and chairs - this looks like the dining room! Let me turn left to face it directly.

messages.content: This looks like a living room, not dining. Let me turn back to find the room with the table I saw earlier.

message.content: This looks like a living room, not dining. Let me turn back to find the room with the table I saw earlier.

messages.content: Now I can see both areas clearly - living room and dining area with table beyond. Let me move toward the dining table area.

message.content: Now I can see both areas clearly - living room and dining area with table beyond. Let me move toward the dining table area.

messages.content: This is clearly a dining room with a large table and chairs. I should be near the entrance now. Let me look around to confirm my position relative to the entrance.

message.content: This is clearly a dining room with a large table and chairs. I should be near the entrance now. Let me look around to confirm my position relative to the entrance.

messages.content: I'm now positioned facing the dining room with the table clearly visible ahead, and I appear to be at the threshold/entrance point between the living area and dining room. This matches the goal description. Let me stop here.

message.content: I'm now positioned facing the dining room with the table clearly visible ahead, and I appear to be at the threshold/entrance point between the living area and dining room. This matches the goal description. Let me stop here.

content.text: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 54 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 46, "tool_calls_remaining": 54}

info.message: STOP not executed (first request). You still have 54 tool calls — verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.

messages.content: My position looks correct - I can see the wooden archway/doorframe behind me (which I just passed through) and the dining room with table ahead. This is the entrance to the dining room. I'll confirm the stop.

message.content: My position looks correct - I can see the wooden archway/doorframe behind me (which I just passed through) and the dining room with table ahead. This is the entrance to the dining room. I'll confirm the stop.
